context.projectOverview: Sprint 5 (FINAL) of spec-20260628-medical-analysis: an EGRESS-GATED online research job. When the literature-retrieval axis is ON, retrieve latest MedlinePlus evidence for the user's markers via the EXISTING LiteratureRetriever/MedlineSource, ground notes through the EXISTING fail-closed grounding-critic synthesis (synthesizeGrounded), write research notes (markdown+frontmatter + citations) into the vault research/ dir, and optionally emit a 'new evidence' watch Finding. With the axis OFF it is a NO-OP with ZERO egress. Exposed as runResearchJob (importable by the scheduler spec) and `bober medical research`. Synthesis fail-closes to the local model unless cloud-inference is on.

completedSprints.title: Proactive trend Findings + vault writer + review pass
completedSprints.summary: src/medical/analysis/{finding,finding-writer,review-pass}.ts. writeFinding + serializeFindingToMarkdown + MedicalFinding + vault dir resolution (config.medical.vaultDir ?? <projectRoot>/.bober/medical/vault). Commit 307e5e7. REUSE the finding-writer + shared vault dir resolution to write the research notes + watch findings; do not rebuild.

notes: Full contract (sc-5-1..sc-5-7, nonGoals, stopConditions, assumptions, estimatedFiles) in .bober/contracts/sprint-spec-20260628-medical-analysis-5.json. Read the Sprint Briefing at .bober/briefings/sprint-spec-20260628-medical-analysis-5-briefing.md FIRST. CRITICAL: (1) with literature-retrieval OFF, runResearchJob returns {disabled:true}, writes nothing, and NEVER constructs/invokes MedlineSource (reuse the synchronous LiteratureRetriever disabled short-circuit at literature.ts:34 as the zero-egress proof). (2) Critic-rejected/abstained topics write NO clinical note (grounding-critic fail-closed) — abstain, do not persist uncited synthesis. (3) Synthesis fail-closes to the LOCAL model via buildMedicalInferenceClient unless cloud-inference is on (no cloud client when off). (4) Do NOT change the reactive engine's existing literature path in engine.ts. runResearchJob is the schedulable entrypoint; the scheduler owns cron/cadence.
